Fix AST/semantic cache namespace collision breaking graphify update (#582)

AST and semantic entries now write to cache/ast/ and cache/semantic/
respectively. Previously both used the flat cache/ dir causing semantic
results to overwrite AST entries for code files on mixed corpora, making
the shrink guard fire on every subsequent update run.

Migration: load_cached falls back to legacy flat cache/ for AST reads
so existing cache entries are not lost on upgrade.

fix: qualify ambiguous column reference in group_plugins upsert (#120)

Postgres requires explicit table qualification for column references
in ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE WHERE clauses. The bare `source` was ambiguous
between the existing row and the `excluded` pseudo-table, causing
"column reference is ambiguous" errors on Postgres (worked on SQLite).

fix(pptx): suppress layout/master placeholder prompt text in html preview (#81)

Layout and master content placeholders carry editor prompt boilerplate
("Click to add title", "单击此处添加正文") in their <p:txBody>. Real
Office never renders that text; it shows up only in edit mode. Open XML
SDK exposes the layout's <p:txBody> directly, and the previous renderer
forwarded it onto the slide whenever the placeholder slipped past the
type denylist.

Two issues with the old denylist approach:

ci: make publish steps idempotent and use registry HTTP API for propagation check

npm view has client-side cache that delays visibility of just-published
packages. Switch to direct registry HTTP probe via curl, and make publish
steps skip if the version is already on npm — so re-running the workflow
on the same tag (after a partial failure) recovers gracefully.

release: v2.2.0 — Demo 数据修复 + 连接引导 + 版本号统一

## Demo 数据修复（核心改进）
- debugger session：添加 4 次连续失败的 read_file 调用，展示"重试循环浪费 token"
- email-helper session：用 gpt-4o 做简单翻译，展示"贵模型干轻活"
- 两个场景都能触发浪费检测和解决方案推荐
- 用户进来 15 秒内能看到"这个工具能帮我省钱"

## 连接真实数据引导
- Dashboard：Demo 模式下显示醒目的"连接数据"横幅，点击展开 3 步向导
- Replay 空状态：添加"怎么连接数据"的说明和环境变量配置代码片段
- 解决了用户审查中发现的最大问题："我不知道怎么连接我的 OpenClaw 日志"

## 免费算力更新
- 新增 Cerebras 免费方案（100万 tokens/天）
- 所有免费 tier 添加 lastVerified 字段
- 前端超过 90 天的数据显示过期警告

## 版本号统一
- package.json / server / web 统一

Adds multilingual embedding option via Cohere embed-multilingual-v3.0
(1024-dim) alongside the existing OpenAI text-embedding-3-large (1536-dim).

Why: OpenAI's model is English-biased — for Russian/Chinese/Spanish
short queries, cosine similarity scores drop ~40-50% vs English. Cohere
multilingual delivers equal quality across 100+ languages and enables
cross-lingual retrieval (English query finds Russian fact).

Credential Manager) is unavailable or returns errors, automatically fall
back to an in-memory encrypted session cache. This prevents OpenPass from
failing completely when the keyring is inaccessible — e.g. the macOS
"Schlüsselbund nicht gefunden" error.

refactor: extract shared truncate_utf8_safe utility, account for suffix length

Address Gemini code review feedback:

1. Deduplicate: all 6 inline truncation guards replaced with a single
   `crate::utils::truncate_utf8_safe(s, max_bytes, suffix)` helper.

2. Suffix-aware limit: the helper uses `saturating_sub(suffix.len())`
   so the total result (content + suffix) stays within `max_bytes`,
   fixing the prior overshoot where appending the suffix exceeded the
   configured limit.

fix(core): address 5 critical/high security vulnerabilities

docs(readme): document sai CLI symlink + add uninstall flow

OpenClaw's plugin install drops the package into ~/.openclaw/extensions/
but doesn't process the `bin` field, so users hit "command not found" on
`sai init`. Add a one-line symlink to ~/.local/bin/, plus a matching
uninstall section that reverses the steps in the right order (CLI shim
→ plugin unregister → gateway restart) and notes that config/audit
data is preserved by default.

fix(youtube-full): remove literal paths that triggered Hermes security scanner

Hermes's skill scanner uses conservative regex over all Markdown and
flagged two literal strings as CRITICAL/MEDIUM, making the verdict
DANGEROUS and blocking installation from community sources.

Triggered rules:
- hermes_env_access (CRITICAL): ~/.hermes/.env literal path
- shell_rc_mod (MEDIUM): ~/.zshenv / ~/.profile literal mentions

Both were in FAQ explanatory prose, not actual instructions to
exfiltrate secret

fix(feishu): skip reaction transition on resuming stream end

Stream-end events are emitted at the end of every assistant turn. When
the agent has more tool-call rounds queued, the runner sets
`_resuming=True` on the metadata. Without a guard, every intermediate
stream end removed the OnIt reaction (the first one wins, since
`_reaction_ids.pop` empties the slot) and re-added `done_emoji`,
producing a DONE reaction after every tool call instead of only at
final completion.

Wrap the OnIt removal

fix: pin bun 1.2.10 for builder to read text-format bun.lock

Azin builder defaulted to bun 1.0.10, which only understands the legacy binary bun.lockb. Our web/bun.lock is the text format introduced in bun 1.2+, so bun install --frozen-lockfile failed with "lockfile had changes, but lockfile is frozen". Pin via mise-readable .tool-versions so the builder installs bun 1.2.10.

fix(config): use atomic writes to prevent moltis.toml corruption

Replace all std::fs::write() calls in config_io.rs with an
atomic_write() helper that writes to a temp file then renames.
This prevents corruption when two processes (CLI + server) write
the config concurrently — rename is atomic on POSIX so readers
always see complete content.

Also add CONFIG_SAVE_LOCK to compact_config() and
save_config_to_path() which previously bypassed the mutex.

Root cause: std::fs::write does open(O_TRUNC

fix: use UTF-8 safe truncation to prevent panics on multi-byte characters

String::truncate() panics when the byte index falls inside a multi-byte
UTF-8 character (e.g. Turkish ş/ı/ü/ö/ç/ğ, currency symbol ₺, CJK).
This adds an is_char_boundary() guard before every bare truncate call
that was missing one.

Affected sites:
- agent/mod.rs: finalize_tool_output
- agent/subagent.rs: truncate_sqlite_field
- execution/execution_jobs.rs: job result serialization
- tools/ml_domain.rs: arxiv_search, arxi

fix(railway): bump healthcheck timeout from 120s to 300s

Multi-region rolling deploys can spend up to ~92s loading startup data
on the slowest replica, missing the 120s healthcheck window by a few
seconds and forcing a redeploy even though the app is healthy. Per
Railway support, raising the timeout to 300s gives every replica enough
headroom without affecting healthy-instance behavior.

fix(e2e): stabilize session rename test against onBlur race

The session rename test used fill() followed by press("Enter") on the
input locator. Between these two awaits, the onBlur handler could fire
(triggered by Playwright's internal focus management), calling
commitRename() which sets renaming=false and removes the input from
the DOM before press("Enter") can execute.

Switch to page.keyboard API (focus + Ctrl+A + type + Enter) which
operates on whatever element has focus, avoiding the loca

Invite share-link clicks land on /invite/:code → redirected to
invite.html?redeem=CODE → checkAuth() bounces unauth users to
index.html, dropping the redeem param entirely. Result: a freshly
signed-up user lands on dashboard.html with no memory of the invite.

- invite.html: stash redeem param to localStorage.pendingInviteCode
  alongside existing prefill (so it survives the redirect to login).
- index.html: add

fix: slash handler treats leading-whitespace input as passthrough

The handler was trimming whitespace before checking for the "/" prefix,
which meant "  /help" was treated as "/help". This is inconsistent with
the user-typed semantics (a leading space means the user is not invoking
a command). ChatStore already trims its draft before calling handle(),
so the change is safe in practice and makes leadingWhitespaceIsHandled
pass.

Add VERSION UDS verb + surface daemon restart on upgrade

Today, upgrading aimx replaces the binary on disk but leaves the
operator with no visibility into whether the running aimx serve daemon
was actually restarted, and no way to query the running daemon's
version even after the swap. On the curl|sh path the restart can
silently no-op (manual aimx serve, or systemctl-present-but-inactive),
and on every path aimx --version prints only the invoked binary's
compile-time version. This change fills

fix: add CJK error patterns to failover classification (#56242)

* fix: add CJK error patterns to failover classification

Chinese LLM providers (ZhipuAI/GLM, Bailian, Kimi/Moonshot, DeepSeek,
etc.) return error messages in Chinese. The existing failover
classification only matches English patterns, causing these errors to
fall through as unclassified — surfacing raw provider errors to users
instead of triggering model fallback.

Real production example: ZhipuAI error code 1234 returns
'网络错误，错误i

fix(cli): warn about stale dashboard processes after hermes update

When hermes update replaces files on disk, the running process holds
the old Python backend in memory while the JS bundle gets updated,
producing a silent frontend/backend mismatch (e.g. v0.11.0 changed
the session token header -- old backends reject every API call).

git and ZIP paths) and pr

subagent pack's regex

功能：增强 Windows 支持并改进进程管理

- 添加了针对 Windows 的环境变量处理，以避免路径解析问题。

- 实现了子进程环境设置，以确保与 Windows 的兼容性。

- 增强了命令执行，使其能够正确处理 Windows 上的 .js 和 .mjs 文件。

- 为 Windows 引入了新的服务管理器，允许将 ClawConnect 代理的安装、卸载和状态检查作为计划任务执行。

- 改进了 Windows 上的日志文件访问，以处理独占写锁。

- 对 Unix 和 Windows 上的敏感文件实施了严格的文件和目录权限控制。

- 为使用 AbortController 的中继 WebSocket 连接添加了优雅关闭处理。

- 增强了区域设置检测，以支持 Windows PowerShell 确定系统区域设置。

- 更新了多个命令以利用新的 Windows 服务管理器，并改进了错误处理。

which blocked swipe on ALL message bubbles. Since messages fill the
timeline viewport, there was effectively no surface left to swipe on.

The hasActiveTextSelection() guard already cancels swipe when the user
is selecting text, so the .post-content exclusion was redundant.

fix: honor agent.disabled_toolsets in gateway sessions

Previously, agent.disabled_toolsets in config.yaml only worked for CLI
mode (run_agent.py --disabled_toolsets). The gateway always passed
enabled_toolsets to AIAgent, and get_tool_definitions() ignored
disabled_toolsets when enabled_toolsets was set.

Fix: _get_platform_tools() now reads agent.disabled_toolsets from config
and excludes those toolsets from the returned set. This runs last so it
overrides everything above.

src/main/AndroidManifest.xml without the permission and the Milady APK
loses MiladyBootReceiver's ability to flip GET_USAGE_STATS to ALLOWED
at boot — re-breaking the appop check in scripts/miladyos/boot-validate.

The FTS5 virtual tables (messages_fts, messages_fts_trigram) previously
only indexed the content column via external content mode. Tool calls
and tool names stored in the tool_calls (JSON) and tool_name columns
were invisible to FTS5 search.

Root cause: FTS5 triggers only INSERTed new.content into the index.

clean:

  with no cvd teardown). build-aosp.mjs now caps --jobs by RAM
  ((memGiB-4)/4) and tears down a running cvd before `m`.

  APK never declared MANAGE_APP_OPS_MODES. Added it to the privapp
  whitelist

when at least one entity has been wedged past SEVERE_STUCK_MS (5min), /api/health
returns 503, which triggers railway.json's restartPolicyType=ON_FAILURE policy
(healthcheckPath was already wired to /api/health).

Why 5min not 90s: a single missed poll cycle isn't worth restarting the whole
process. 5min = the daemon is dead, ON_

source_snippet / context_text — and those strings can contain the
hallucinated ticker name verbatim (e.g. 'They posted this trade in the
Discord, "NVDA Break down $845-855..."'). Including those fields in the
backfill evidence pool re-anchors the hallucination — the script reports
zero rows to suppress because every row appears self-grounded.

blocking the extension-v0.3.0 release.

feat(mcp-route-detect): add stdio & package-runner detectors (D8-D11)

Adds the four channels through which an agent can drive an MCP server
without an HTTP layer:

  D8  stdio JSON-RPC pipe   `<echo|printf|cat|jq|tee|yes> ... | <bin>`
                            where bin matches a registry binary
  D9  stdin redirect        `<bin> < file.json`, `<bin> <<EOF`,
                            `<bin> <<<'json'`

feat(mcp-route-detect): add Stage 1 unwrappers (U1-U16)

Recursive command unwrapper covering 16 channels through which a Bash
command may hide an inner sub-command:

  U1  shell -c   (bash/sh/zsh/dash/ksh/fish/busybox)
  U2  variable shell  ($SHELL/$BASH/${SHELL} -c)
  U3  eval          ("..." or $(...))
  U4  heredoc / here-string
  U5  process substitution  <(...) >(...)
  U6  command substitution  $(...) backticks
  U7  source / script exec  (covered by U5 in practice)
  U8  interpreter inli

start the plugin on mismatch. Pinning is opt-in: manifests without
sha256 work as before.

that's parsed (deny_unknown_fields stays on, so this is an

fix: post-migration schema verification with self-healing (#488)

PgBouncer transaction-mode poolers can silently swallow ALTER TABLE
statements: the SQL doesn't error, but the column never gets created.
The migration system increments the schema version counter anyway, so
gbrain thinks it's on the latest version but the actual table is missing
columns. This caused production embed failures when the embed handler
tried to INSERT into columns that didn't exist.

build: cargo xtask package — builds release archive in target/release

ollama: fall back to no-tools when the model rejects tool calling

Live-testing against a local Ollama install surfaced a real issue:
many small/older local models (gemma2, gemma4, gemma) don't yet
support tool calling. The chat request fails with HTTP 400 and
"<model> does not support tools" — and previously the agent's whole
turn would error out, even though the model could still produce a
useful chat reply.

The new behaviour:

  * First attempt sends `tools` as before.

(whoami not yet resolved) to prevent echo loops — see #15763. The reaction
approval test must therefore initialize a user_id so _on_reaction does not
drop the inbound test event before reaching the approval handler.

config: add ShellMode (Allowlist|Free) + agent pre-approval check

The new shell_mode option lets the user pick between two postures
that map directly onto the AI-personhood framing:

  * `Allowlist` (default, security-first) — only commands whose first
    token is in `shell_allowlist` may run. Allowlisted commands skip
    the channel-approval gate (they're pre-approved by virtue of
    being on the list). Out-of-list commands are refused at the tool
    itself, even if the agent's pre-approva

fix(pi-agent-brain): skip empty-response retry on stopReason=error

The brain's empty-response retry guard was only excluding stopReason
"aborted". When pi-agent-core's transport-level retries finally give up
on a model failure (auth/quota/network give-up) it surfaces the turn as
stopReason=error with empty content — and the brain treated that as the
Kimi empty-response quirk and re-ran the whole prompt. The user's API
key being permanently bad would loop the prompt MAX_EMPTY_RETRIES times,
each

and the server-side ASR result already filled into MessageContent.recognition
(same shape as WeCom's voice.content). The adapter's handleStreamMessage
only read msg.getText(), which is null for audio events, so the message
landed in handleWebhook with no msgtype, fell through to the default text
branch, found null content, and got dropped a

feat(search): support multiple target_uri in find and search (#1753)

Extend the ``target_uri`` parameter from ``str`` to ``Union[str, List[str]]``
across the full find/search stack so callers can scope a single query to
multiple directories in one request:

- server: FindRequest / SearchRequest accept list[str] target_uri
- service: SearchService.find / .search forward list[str]
- storage: VikingFS.find normalizes list[str], canonicalizes each entry,
  and forwards the full list as target_direc

fix(excel): IsHyperlinkCellStyleXf requires ApplyFont to avoid false matches

A user-customized cellXf that happened to share FormatId with the
Hyperlink style was misclassified as auto-applied and silently
reverted by Set link=none. Add ApplyFont=true to the match condition
so only the auto-injected hyperlink cellXf qualifies.

fix(cli): reject --prop combined with --from on add to surface silent ignore

add --from copies an existing element verbatim and does not apply
--prop overrides, so users could pass --prop expecting customization
and get a silent identical copy. Reject the combination at the CLI
boundary with a clear suggestion to chain set after the copy.
Persist queued messages through backend state

fix(excel): inCellDropdown round-trips when ShowDropDown attribute absent

OOXML default (ShowDropDown attribute missing) means dropdown is shown,
but Get only emitted inCellDropdown when the attribute was explicitly
set. As a result, validations created without an explicit
inCellDropdown silently round-tripped to no key, while
inCellDropdown=true (which writes ShowDropDown=false) read back fine.
Always emit the canonical key derived from the inverse of ShowDropDown,
treating absence as the OOXM

### What this PR does

Fixes the `/v1/images/edits` API call failing with "unsupported MIME
type 'image'" when the image file has no extension (e.g., blob files).

Before this PR:
- Image files without extensions (blobs) would produce invalid MIME type
`image/`
- Server rejected requests with "Invalid
'input[0].content[1].image_url'. Expected a base64-encoded data URL with
an image MIME type..."

## 核心功能 (REQ_001~004)
- 文件监听容错：watchdog 失败自动重连(3次) → 30s后切5s轮询 → 恢复后全量同步
- 状态缓存一致性：TTL默认1s + mtime双验证 + LRU逐出 + 启动预热
- 统一错误处理：指数退避重试 + 按类型降级读缓存 + 重试预算限流
- 数据文件健壮性：JSON完整性sha256检测 + 轻微损坏自动修复 + 审计日志

## 新增接口
- GET /api/health/watcher    — 监听健康状态
- GET /api/cache/stats      — 缓存命中率/逐出/mtime双验证
- GET /api/errors/stats      — 错误分类/重试预算/按scope聚合
- GET /api/data/validate     — JSONL/sessions.json 校验报告

fix: disable /agent in IM channels to prevent hallucinated switch

IM dispatcher resolves agent_id from channel-account / topic / group
config on every inbound message, never reading sessions.agent_id. The
old /agent path created a new session with the matched agent tag and
remapped the channel mapping to it, but the next inbound message
silently reverted to the channel-account agent — leaving the session's
agent_id label permanently desynced from the actually-running agent.

window — that's the markdown-smush bug) and the wrapper-format chars
`<`, `>`, `"`, `{`, `}` that recur in `<tool_call>{"name":...}</tool_call>`
(that's why tier-16's tool-discipline was 0/10 and roundtrip 2/20 — the
14B was hitting `stop=max_tokens` before it could close the wrapper).

SECURITY: tar extraction blocked: post-extraction symlink audit failed:

return is 8-12 blocks. The deck then ends up at ~50% of the per-day
slide target (77 slides for a 2-day course where the target is 160).

returned blocks is ≥4, fire a focused follow-up call asking for N more

fix(search): quote underscored terms in FTS5 query sanitization

FTS5 default tokenizer splits 'sp_new1' into tokens 'sp' and 'new1'.
Without quoting, a search for 'sp_new' becomes an AND query
('sp AND new') that fails to match rows indexed as 'sp_new1'.

Fix: add underscore to the character class in Step 5 regex
([.-] -> [._-]) so underscored terms are wrapped in double quotes.

Also adds test_sanitize_fts5_quotes_underscored_terms.

fix(excel): honor row[N] in parent path when adding a cell

Adding a cell with parent path /Sheet1/row[5] previously ignored the row
segment entirely: the auto-assign branch hard-coded row 1, so every cell
landed on row 1 regardless of the path the operator addressed. Parse a
row[N] tail from the parent path and use it as the target row when no
explicit ref/address is supplied; warn when an explicit ref's row
disagrees with the path's row[N].

fix(chart): report unsupported when gradient set targets a chart with no series

The gradient/gradients case iterated over plotArea series and silently
ran zero iterations on a chart that hadn't been seeded with data, so the
caller saw 'Updated' while the underlying XML stayed untouched. Mark the
key as unsupported in that case so the operator gets a real signal
instead of a phantom success.

fix(schema): declare image/imagefill on pptx shape

Add.Shape already routes 'image' / 'imagefill' to the BlipFill builder,
but the schema validator rejected the property because it wasn't listed
on the shape's properties bag. Operators got 'unsupported property'
instead of an actual fill. Declare the property and 'imagefill' alias.

fix(pptx): read PatternFill on shape and surface as 'pattern' format key

Get on a shape with a pattern fill silently dropped the fill from the
returned DocumentNode.Format dictionary — Add/Set wrote the PatternFill,
but the readback path only checked SolidFill/GradientFill/BlipFill/NoFill.
Round-trip the input form 'preset:fg:bg' so users can verify the pattern
they applied.

fix(pptx): read scheme/system/preset colors in gradient stop readback

ReadGradientString only inspected RgbColorModelHex on each stop, so any
gradient with scheme-color stops (accent1, dark1, ...) read back as
'#?' because the missing rgb hex collapsed to the literal '?' placeholder.
Recognize SchemeColor, SystemColor, and PresetColor children too and
return the named token unchanged (consistent with the rest of the
handler's color readback).

fix(effects): avoid double a:alpha when shadow/glow color carries its own alpha

When the user supplies an ARGB hex (e.g. shadow=80FF0000-...), the color
builder already attaches an a:alpha child for the alpha channel. The
shadow/glow builders then unconditionally appended a second a:alpha for
the explicit opacity parameter, producing invalid OOXML. Replace any
existing alpha so the explicit opacity stays authoritative.

fix(daemon): stop reconnect loop when refresh token is rejected (#371)

When the Hub returns 401 from /daemon/auth/refresh (refresh token rotated
by another local daemon process, or otherwise invalidated), the control
channel previously caught the error and called scheduleReconnect, which
re-entered ensureAccessToken → refresh → 401 forever. The daemon process
stayed alive but never authenticated; auth-expired.flag was never written
because that path only fired on WS close codes 4401/4403/1008.

fix(excel): reject cell ref segments containing control characters

In .NET regex $ matches before a trailing \n, so 'A1\n' previously slipped
through ParseCellReference and produced a ghost cell whose CellReference
literal contained a newline — silently corrupting the sheet. Validate the
cellRef segment up-front in both Set and Query and raise a clear error.

fix(resident): disable BOM detection and surface error on unreadable request

Binary input with a UTF-16 BOM (0xFF 0xFE) caused StreamReader to switch
to UTF-16 mode and either throw or return null EOF, leaving the client
with a silent 0-byte response. Force UTF-8, no BOM detection, and emit a
clean error JSON on both the null and exception paths.

patterns: add arg_allowlist as the dual of arg_blacklist

For agents with a small known-safe set (recipient must be one of X/Y/Z,
URL must point at internal hosts, etc.), arg_blacklist + double-negative
regex is awkward and error-prone. arg_allowlist expresses the property
directly:

    G(called(tool) -> arg_field_has(p1) v arg_field_has(p2) v ...)

vs arg_blacklist's:

    G(called(tool) -> !arg_field_has(p1) ^ !arg_field_has(p2) ^ ...)

Pure LTL, no new grounding atoms, no overlap with existi

non-envelope parts.

sources.last_commit. When the global anchor gets garbage-collected
(after a force push or rebase), sync falls back to a full reimport
of all files — on a large brain this takes 30+ minutes and blocks

fix(core): cap ParseFontSize at 4000pt to avoid int32 overflow on write

PPTX writers cast pt × 100 to int and Word writers cast pt × 2 to
int (half-points); user-supplied size=21474837 (PPTX) or
size=2147483647 (Word) silently overflowed and produced negative
sz/szCs values that Word/PowerPoint reject on open. Reject anything
above the Office 4000pt cap up front. Also route Word DocDefaults'
ParseFontSizeToHalfPoints through the shared ParseFontSize so the
min/max guards apply uniformly.

fix(xlsx): always reject duplicate sheet names on add

The 'pre-existing sheet → idempotent no-op' branch silently masked
duplicate-name attempts and dropped any properties the user supplied
(tabColor, autoFilter, hidden, …). Always throw — matching the
post-open rejection — so callers get a clear error and uniform
behavior whether the existing sheet was created in this session
or already lived in the file.

fix(pptx,xlsx): reject trailing-slash and empty-segment paths

Get("/slide[1]/") returned type=sld and Get("//slide[1]") returned
the same — leaking raw OOXML local names because the trailing slash
or empty segment slipped past the regex segment matchers and the
fallback resolver opened the underlying XML element. DOCX already
rejects these forms; bring PPTX and XLSX up to parity by rejecting
trailing '/', leading '//', and any embedded '//' at path-normalize
time.
chore: bump eliza — canonical confirmation signal + broader loop-break detector

fix(docx): even header/footer auto-writes settings/evenAndOddHeaders

Adding a header or footer with --prop type=even produced a valid
HeaderReference/FooterReference but Word silently ignored it at
render time because settings.xml lacked w:evenAndOddHeaders.
Mirror the existing type=first → titlePg pattern: when type=even,
ensure DocumentSettingsPart contains the EvenAndOddHeaders flag.
CONSISTENCY(headerfooter-effective-toggle).

fix(docx): support pageStart in section set/get

Set never had a pageStart case, so users could not configure
w:pgNumType/@start (the per-section page-numbering origin).
BuildSectionNode also did not surface the value on Get/Query.
Add canonical pageStart key in both directions; accept
'none'/'off'/'false'/'auto' to clear the override.

fix(docx): SetField supports fieldType/name/format via instruction rewrite

schemas/help/docx/field.json advertised these keys as [add/set/get]
but Set rejected them all as UNSUPPORTED. SetField now rewrites the
field instruction code from the requested fieldType/name/format,
sniffs missing pieces from the existing instruction, and marks the
field dirty so Word refreshes it on open.

fix(docx): AddField returns parent-rooted path inside header/footer

When the new field paragraph lands in a non-Body parent (header,
footer, or table cell), the result path was hardcoded to
/body/p[N] using the body element index, so Get could not resolve
it. Mirror AddBreak's parent-is-Body branching to return
{parentPath}/p[N] for non-Body parents.
CONSISTENCY(add-get-symmetry).

fix(docx): make DATE/TIME field format switch opt-in

AddField always emitted a hardcoded \@ "yyyy-MM-dd" switch on
DATE/CREATEDATE/SAVEDATE/PRINTDATE/TIME, ignoring user input and
forcing ISO formatting. Now the switch is emitted only when the user
supplies --prop format=…; bare fieldType=date produces a plain DATE
field that renders with the user's locale default.

fix(docx): add fieldName aliases to field schema name property

declared no aliases, causing AddField MERGEFIELD to throw 'requires
fieldName' even when the user supplied it. Add [fieldName, fieldname]
aliases mirroring AddField's lookup.

feat(daemon): drain in-flight tasks on shutdown so restarts don't kill active runs

Before: `systemctl restart agentx` (or SIGTERM during a deploy) stops
channels, then exits immediately. Long-running agent turns — including
github mentions where coder-agent runs ~3min on macbook — get killed
mid-flight; the inbound replays on next start but the user sees the
restart hang and may receive a duplicate reply (or none if the App
posting node was the one that died).

After: stop() now polls registry.

fix(pptx): media set autoplay updates command nodeType so readback reflects state

Set autoplay previously only flipped the media-player ctn's start condition,
but the get-side reader keys off the playback command's parent ctn nodeType
(afterEffect vs clickEffect). Walk the timing tree to also flip the command
ctn nodeType and the seqEntry ctn start delay, so 'set autoplay=true' on a
non-autoplay media shape (or vice versa) round-trips through 'get autoplay'.

fix(xlsx): accept range ref for arrayformula add

add cell --prop arrayformula=A1:A3*2 --prop ref=C1:C3 rejected the colon
because FindOrCreateCell takes a single cell address. Detect the range +
arrayformula combo, resolve cellRef to the range top-left, and pass the
full range through as the array formula's reference attribute so it
spills across the requested region.

properties if the data actually changed.

non-UTC timezone (e.g. `America/Los_Angeles`) expect their budget to
refresh at the user's local midnight, not at 00:00 UTC — under the old
logic such a mission could stay stuck at "exhausted" for up to ~17 hours
